essence: Held. Heirloom is the loom that keeps a family's voices woven together across generations — every life is a thread, the family is the cloth, and the cloth is held for as long as the family lasts.
promise: You keep the conversation going. We keep it safe, and we make sure it reaches the people you meant it for — written, spoken, and passed on, for as long as your family lasts.
positioningLine: Some things are meant to be kept. (Supporting line, used after the hero, not as the hero: "Keep your family's story going — across generations." The legacy "thousand-year thread" is demoted to a single quiet durability proof-point, never the headline.)
personalityVoice: Warm, present-tense, second person, addressed to a real named person. The voice of someone alive at the kitchen table at golden hour — concrete, sensory (hands, weather, food, a kitchen, a name), occasionally funny, occasionally aching, never bleak and never saccharine. Plainspoken and certain without being clinical. We say "keep" and "pass on," not "death," "legacy data," or "memorialize." Reverent at the sealed/release moments, never funereal. Premium but unintimidating — a 34-year-old new parent and their 78-year-old grandmother both find it beautiful and easy.
distinctiveAssets: The Warp-and-Weft mark: a single structural over-under woven cell, never a glowing node or filament web. Ownable because it is the product's data model (many threads, one cloth) made into a glyph.

Your colour for life: at joining, each member chooses one of the 10 named natural dyes; it becomes their name colour and 3px left warp-margin thread everywhere, forever. The onboarding ritual is 'choosing your thread.' Nobody else has this memory hook.

The Family Spectrum: the unique woven colour-bar of dyes a specific family contains — a coat of arms generated from real data. Headers their Book cover (foil bands), share cards, and anniversary email footer. Every family's brand is literally theirs alone.

Dyes named, not numbered — madder, cochineal, kermes, saffron, weld, walnut, oakgall, woad, indigo, iron. Pigments with centuries of history and meaning (joy, love, grief, daily, ending) instead of hex codes. A verbal + chromatic asset at once.

The Listener: one quiet daily prompt as the signature content unit across product, social, and email — and a call-and-response engine that resurfaces a real family member's past entry to answer across time ('Your grandmother wrote about this kitchen in 1979 — what do you remember?').

The mono archival stamp: dates, generation counts, keeper lineage and thread IDs in uppercase tracked mono — the same 'this is a record that outlives you' watermark from the website footer to the printed Book colophon.

Reverent release lexicon: a fixed, gentle vocabulary set in the display serif — 'held until', 'opens when', 'for Maya, when she turns 30', 'passed on to you by'. Dignity rendered as a repeatable, designed asset. No countdowns, locks, or skulls.
visualSystem: Three honest materials: woven structure, dyed thread, warm paper light. Layout is a literal warp grid — an 8px base rhythm (4px sub-grid) where content threads hang off a left warp line. Single reading column, max 66ch measure for prose, ≥17px body everywhere (multigenerational legibility is non-negotiable; never the old 70%-empty unreadable vault). Negative space is structure but inhabited: target ~55–60% empty, warm and tactile, not austere. The 3D cloth/weave surface remains the home plane; UI sits on a warm paper plane above it with generous quiet margins. Material references stay abstract and premium — real woven-cloth macro photography and a genuine warp/weft motion language; never yarn balls, knitting needles, samplers, cartoon spools, glassmorphism, conic-gradient mesh, animated noise, glowing filament webs, or AI 'organic blob' art. Dyes appear ONLY as the 3px warp margin + name colour — never as fills, buttons, or backgrounds, or the palette becomes a clown grid. The ember accent appears at under 5% of any surface, reserved for the live present moment only.
colorSystem:
Two warm grounds, one emotional accent, ten signal dyes. Defined in OKLCH for both themes, hex given for build.

DARK (default — 'ink'):
- ink ground #0b0907; abyss #090706; surface #16110c; elevated #1e1812; hover #261e15.
- bone (primary text) #f2e6d0; bone-bright #faf3e4; bone-dim rgba(242,230,208,0.72); bone-faint rgba(242,230,208,0.44); rule rgba(242,230,208,0.11).
- EMBER (the one accent — copper-amber, 'now'): #e0a062, bright #f0c074, dim #b07a3e, shadow #8a5a2a. Under 5% surface area, reserved for: the Listener prompt, the active compose cursor, the single primary CTA, and the convergence pip in the mark.
- blood (destructive only, muted into the dye world, never bright-red 2026 tell): #9f3a2a.

LIGHT ('vellum'):
- vellum ground oklch(0.96 0.014 75) ≈ #f6ecda; text ink #0b0907; copper must use the AA-lifted light family (the dual --warm / --loom-warm tokens both raised) so the accent clears 4.5:1 on vellum.

THE 10 DYES (signal only; each ships a paired --dye / --dye-text token so the vivid thread reads as a 3px margin and the AA-tuned text variant holds 4.5:1 in BOTH grounds): madder (joy), cochineal (grief), kermes (love), saffron (achievement), weld (daily), walnut (travel), oakgall (record), woad (contemplation), indigo (reflection), iron (ending). For print, map dye OKLCH to a CMYK-safe profile — the iron/woad/indigo trio require a verified press proof.
typeSystem:
Two variable families plus one mono; drop Cormorant entirely (it is the dated vault tell).

- DISPLAY / VOICE: Fraunces (variable) — opsz pushed to ~110–120 at hero, weight ~360, SOFT 0 / WONK 0, tracking -0.01em. Emotional surface only: hero lines, the Listener prompt, sealed/release copy, chapter openers. Never below its ~24px optical floor; never used for running body.
- READING / PROSE / INPUTS: Source Serif 4 (variable) at 17px / 1.6 — so a hundred-year-old letter reads like a letter, not an app. This is the memory-body workhorse.
- UI CHROME (buttons, nav, form labels, small product UI): Inter (variable) — tall x-height, calm, durable.
- ARCHIVAL / METADATA: a single mono — JetBrains Mono — UPPERCASE, +0.12em tracking, used sparingly for dates, generation counts, keepership roles, entry IDs, the colophon and OG footer stamp. Tabular numerals everywhere a date or count appears.

Type scale fluid (clamp-based, ~1.5 ratio at display, ~1.2 in body). Wordmark itself: Fraunces, opsz ~110, weight ~360, all lowercase except a capital H — 'Heirloom'.
markBrief:
THE WARP-AND-WEFT MARK — a single woven cell, structural, never a glowing node or filament web.

MASTER ICON / PWA (viewBox 0 0 192 192):
- Ground: solid ink #0b0907, full bleed.
- Three vertical WARP threads, round-cap strokes weight 9.6px, in bone #f2e6d0, at x = 64, 96, 128, each running y = 28 → 164.
- Two horizontal WEFT threads at y = 72 and y = 120, round-cap strokes weight 9.6px, also bone — but each weft INTERLACES: drawn as two segments with a gap where it passes BEHIND the centre warp (x=96), and passing IN FRONT of the two outer warps. So each weft = a left segment from x=44→~88 (in front of x=64 warp), then a ~16px gap across x=96, then a right segment from ~104→148 (in front of x=128 warp). The centre warp visibly crosses over both wefts at x=96; the outer warps pass under. This makes a true over-under woven 3×2 cell.
- THE EMBER KNOT at the held centre intersection (96,96): a soft radial halo r=40 in ember #e0a062 at ~0.4 alpha behind a solid ember pip r=12 (#f0c074 core blending to #e0a062). This is the only colour in the mark and the only 'glow' — a single warm heartbeat at the join, not a lens flare.

FAVICON (viewBox 0 0 48 48) — simplify so the interlace still reads at 16px: TWO warps at x=18 and x=30 (stroke 3.2px, y=8→40) and ONE weft at y=24 (stroke 3.2px) that passes in front of the left warp, gaps across centre, passes in front of the right warp — i.e. an over-under cross — plus the ember pip r=3 at (24,24) with a faint r=8 halo. Reads as a woven knot, never a generic hash or '#'.

MASKABLE TILE: ink bleeds to the edge; the entire weave + ember knot lives inside the inner 80% safe circle, geometrically centred so the ember pip sits at tile centre and survives iOS squircle + Android circle masks.

REVERSED (on vellum): warps/wefts in ink #0b0907, ember knot unchanged — works on the light theme and on the Book cover deboss.

WORDMARK + LOCKUP: 'Heirloom' in Fraunces (opsz ~110, weight ~360, lowercase except capital H, tracking -0.01em). Horizontal lockup = mark tile at left, mark height set equal to the cap-height of the H, optical gap ≈ 0.6× cap-height between mark and wordmark. The two adjacent 'o's may optionally be rendered as two small woven cells echoing the mark — a quiet, never-required flourish.
motionSystem: One calm spring, physics-based — stiffness ~170, damping ~26 — for entrances and layout; micro-states reduce to a 220ms ease. Threads settle, they never bounce. Durations cluster at 180 / 360 / 720 / 1400ms. Every motion carries information: a thread being woven into the warp (a new entry/member joining), a weft folding under (a note sealing), the hero cloth drawing one ember weft across on load (~1400ms) then resting. Nothing loops forever — the cloth breathes once and stops. THE SEAL gesture is the one moment of deliberate ceremony: a slow press-and-hold / fold that feels like pressing wax, gravity in the hand not in a warning banner — no ember urgency, no countdown. prefers-reduced-motion is fully honoured (sections appear without weaving; the cloth renders settled).
voiceRules: Lead every sensitive moment with arrival-of-love, never loss: 'for Maya, when she turns 30', not 'after you're gone'. Death is the mechanism in the architecture; it is never the foreground emotion or in any acquisition copy.
Present tense, second person, name a real person. 'Tell your daughter what the kitchen smelled like' beats 'preserve a memory'. Sensory and concrete over abstract.

Use the kept-vocabulary: keep, pass on, held until, opens when, choose your thread, the Listener, the Bloodline, keeper/Successor, woven in. Banish: memorialize, legacy data, deceased, vault, secure your legacy, before it's too late.

No urgency, ever — no countdowns, scarcity, streak guilt, 'you haven't written in 5 days', FOMO, or A/B pressure. Forbidden absolutely near sealing, verification, release, and pricing. The thousand-year promise dies the instant the product behaves like quarterly-growth SaaS.
No gamification of memory — no badges, points, completion %, leaderboards. Reinforcement is relational and warm: 'Maya read your kitchen story', never '+1, 3-day streak'.
The dead-man's-switch / verification speaks as calm caretaking: 'A quiet note — just confirming all is well. No action needed if it is. Take your time.' Infrequent, reversible, reverent.

The after-passing release email is the most carefully made object in the system: reverent, no marketing, no app chrome, no ember, recipient controls WHEN they open it. 'Someone left this for you. It opens now.'
Soft CTAs on social: 'ask someone tonight', never 'sign up now'. No grief content, no estate-planning angle, no death-awareness-month.

Keep AI as a clearly-labelled midwife to the user's own words — gentle prompts, narration, dye suggestions — never a ventriloquist. Never generate a 'memory' or voice in a real (especially deceased) person's voice.
decisions: MARK: All three lenses wanted to kill both the bare infinity glyph AND the cosmic glowing-filament web. uxart's structural warp-and-weft cell wins outright over brand's simpler single-crossing thread — it is more ownable and more legible at 512px — but I adopt the psych/brand warning and keep the ember 'glow' to a single tiny knot pip (under the anti-cliché rule), not a radial node. Resolved to uxart's geometry with brand's discipline.

TYPE: brand proposed Inter Tight + Fraunces; uxart proposed Fraunces + Source Serif 4 + Inter + mono. I take uxart's fuller stack because the product has TWO distinct text jobs (emotional voice vs. hundred-year reading body) that one sans cannot serve. Fraunces for voice, Source Serif 4 for prose/inputs, Inter for chrome, JetBrains Mono for the archival stamp. Cormorant is dropped by unanimous agreement.

TAGLINE: psych argued 'thousand-year' is cold and boastful; uxart wanted to keep a sharpened version; brand proposed 'Some things are meant to be kept.' I make 'Some things are meant to be kept.' the master hero line (most emotional, least boastful), keep brand's relational 'keep the conversation going across generations' as the working sub-line, and demote 'thousand-year thread' to a single quiet durability proof-point — not deleted (it is in-market and the commercial vocabulary references it) but never the headline.

DYES: promoted from hidden data-layer to public brand spectrum (brand's big idea) BUT held to signal-only rendering — 3px warp margin + AA-tuned name text, never fills/buttons (uxart + the existing globals.css dual-token discipline). This resolves 'make them the brand' against 'don't make a clown grid'.

EMBER vs DYE hierarchy: the one copper-amber accent = NOW (the live present, the call to write, the mark's knot); the family's own dyes = WHO. Past and sealed content is quiet bone/dye. This gives a single unambiguous accent rule across product, email, social, and Book.

NEGATIVE SPACE: resolved the legacy 70%-austere-vault against multigenerational legibility — target ~55–60% empty, warm and inhabited, ≥17px body, AA everywhere. Warmth and legibility beat severity because a 78-year-old grandmother is a primary reader.

SEAL / RELEASE: rendered as cloth (a folded weft, a thread woven in) with mono date labels — never padlocks, tombstones, hourglasses, countdowns, or 'deceased' language. The ceremony lives in a press-and-hold gesture (weight in the hand), the dignity in fixed reverent copy.

BOOK: all three converge — the physical archival hardcover is the proof-of-permanence and the answer to 'what if the company is gone'. Spec'd as the same warp grid, dye margins, mono colophon, and the family Spectrum on the foil-stamped cover.
doNot: No countdown timers, scarcity, streaks, FOMO, or urgency anywhere — categorically forbidden near sealing, the dead-man's-switch, release, and pricing.
Never lead with death, loss, tombstones, hourglasses, padlocks, 'deceased', 'memorialize', or 'before it's too late'. Death lives in the architecture, never the foreground.
No glowing radial node, converging filament web, or sci-fi 'cosmic loom' mark — that is the retired 2026 AI cliché being replaced.
No glassmorphism, frosted/blurred translucency, conic-gradient mesh, animated noise, 3D bevels, or AI 'organic blob' shapes.
No Cormorant Garamond, and never set running body in the display serif or drop the display serif below ~24px.
No dye colours as fills, buttons, or backgrounds — signal only (3px warp margin + name text).
No icon libraries or decorative emoji; the warp-and-weft mark and the mono stamp carry recognition.
No avatar circles / rounded-full identity chips, no loading spinners (use the hairline ProgressHair), no toast notifications (inline status only).
No gamification of memory — no badges, points, completion %, leaderboards, or read-receipts framed as performance.
No performance pressure at the Composer — no audience counters, no AI quality-scoring of entries, no 'great memories get more reads'.
No AI ventriloquism — never generate words or a voice impersonating a real or deceased person; AI is a labelled midwife only.
Never A/B-pressure or alter the frozen pricing (Free $0 / Family $6.99·mo $69·yr / Founder $249 role-only, withdrawn from sale); ZAR localization is untouchable.
No stock photography in email; no marketing chrome or ember on the after-passing release email.
Do not reintroduce the austere 70%-empty monastic vault or tiny unreadable type — it alienates the multigenerational audience.
